## ASSISTANT

All checks pass. Here's a summary of what was fixed for each finding:

| Finding | What changed |
|---------|-------------|
| **6** | `_recompute` in `covenant_state_service.py` now raises `CovenantCalculationError` when `new_denominator == 0`, preventing a raw `ZeroDivisionError` from leaking to callers |
| **7** | `ingest()` in `asset_ingestion_service.py` captures one `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` per batch and passes it to `_build_record`, so all records in a batch share an identical `ingested_at` |
| **8** | `update_covenant_state()` short-circuits on empty contributions — calls `repo.get()` (no lock) and returns early without writing, avoiding unnecessary lock contention and spurious writes |
| **4** | `upsert()` in `postgres_facility_covenant_state_repository.py` wraps the INSERT path in a `begin_nested()` SAVEPOINT and converts `IntegrityError` → `CovenantPublicationError`, so a concurrent first-insert race leaves the outer transaction intact |
| **9** | Removed the unused `repository: CovenantReportRepository = Depends(get_repository)` parameter from `get_publisher()` in `dependencies.py` |
| **10** | Removed `fee_amount: Decimal` from `NominaAsset` and its corresponding read from `NominaMapper.map()` |
| **11** | Removed `outstanding_fee_amount: Decimal` from `PayEarlyAsset` and its corresponding read from `PayEarlyMapper.map()` |
| **12** | Added composite index `ix_covenant_reports_facility_computed (facility_id, computed_at)` on `covenant_reports`; removed the now-redundant single-column `index=True` on `facility_id` |
| **13** | Added composite index `ix_assets_facility_ingested (facility_id, ingested_at)` on `assets` |
| **14** | Deleted dead `_nullable_decimal` function and removed the unused `Optional` import from `postgres_asset_repository.py` |
| **15** | Deleted `app/interfaces/api/schemas/request.py` (contained only the unreferenced `CalculateCovenantRequest`) |

```
9	The sum of per-asset contribution_numerator values in the DB is not identical
10	to the in-memory Decimal sum used when the state was built (Numeric(30,10)
11	truncation means individually-stored values sum differently than the full-
12	precision accumulation). Storing the state snapshot removes that discrepancy.

35	def downgrade() -> None:
```

```json
{"file_path":"/home/antonio/Documentos/fence/alembic/versions/0007_add_composite_indexes.py","content":"\"\"\"Add composite indexes for facility+timestamp queries.\n\nRevision ID: 0007\nRevises: 0006\nCreate Date: 2026-06-14\n\nReplaces the single-column ix_covenant_reports_facility_id with a composite\n(facility_id, computed_at) index — the primary access pattern is always\nscoped to a facility and ordered by report date.\n\nAdds a parallel composite (facility_id, ingested_at) index on assets for the\nsame reason: report generation and verification filter by facility and order\nby ingestion time.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sqlalchemy as sa\nfrom alembic import op\n\nrevision = \"0007\"\ndown_revision = \"0006\"\nbranch_labels = None\ndepends_on = None\n\n\ndef upgrade() -> None:\n    # covenant_reports: drop redundant single-column index, add composite one\n    op.drop_index(\"ix_covenant_reports_facility_id\", table_name=\"covenant_reports\")\n    op.create_index(\n        \"ix_covenant_reports_facility_computed\",\n        \"covenant_reports\",\n        [\"facility_id\", \"computed_at\"],\n    )\n\n    # assets: add composite index alongside the existing single-column one\n    op.create_index(\n        \"ix_assets_facility_ingested\",\n        \"assets\",\n        [\"facility_id\", \"ingested_at\"],\n    )\n\n\ndef downgrade() -> None:\n    op.drop_index(\"ix_assets_facility_ingested\", table_name=\"assets\")\n    op.drop_index(\n        \"ix_covenant_reports_facility_computed\", table_name=\"covenant_reports\"\n    )\n    op.create_index(\n        \"ix_covenant_reports_facility_id\", \"covenant_reports\", [\"facility_id\"]\n    )\n"}
```

## ASSISTANT

Everything is clean. Here's what was found and done:

**Alembic version:** 1.18.4 installed — no issue, `pyproject.toml` pins `>=1.13.0`.

**Missing migration:** The review fixes for findings 12 & 13 changed the models but had no corresponding migration. The new `0007_add_composite_indexes.py` fills the gap:

| Operation | Why |
|-----------|-----|
| DROP `ix_covenant_reports_facility_id` | The model no longer has `index=True` on `covenant_reports.facility_id` — the composite index replaces it |
| CREATE `ix_covenant_reports_facility_computed (facility_id, computed_at)` | The real query pattern: per-facility report listing ordered by date |
| CREATE `ix_assets_facility_ingested (facility_id, ingested_at)` | Supports `find_by_facility_at()` which filters by facility and timestamp; also speeds up report generation |

The old `ix_assets_facility_id` (single-column) is **kept** because the model still has `index=True` on that column and it covers `find_existing_external_ids()` queries that only filter by `facility_id`.
